title: Modified Frame-projected  Independent Fiber  Photometry (FIP) System_Hardware
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ajjn6","text":"This is a step-by-step protocol to build a modified FIP (Frame-projected Independent Fiber Photometry) system. FIP was first implemented and reported by Kim et al. (2016). Their protocol is available here.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":200,"length":4}],"data":{}},{"key":"3eln1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dlto5","text":"We have modified the previous design so that it can be built primarily with Thorlabs products (some products from other manufacturers are still required), which are off-the-shelf and easy to obtain with a reasonable lead time. The system described in this protocol is designed to record signals from 1) GFP-based sensors, 2) mApple-based sensors, and 3) isosbestic signals from up to 4-9 sites depending on fiber bundle selection (Fig. 1 and 2). When applied to measurements of other fluorescent protein-based sensors or spectrally shifted sensors, the selection of optical filters and/or excitation light sources should be modified accordingly. Also, the selection of the fiber patch-cable should be based on the number of simultaneously recorded sites and the diameter/NA of the fiber implants.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":431,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fd13h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"031BE3601C1E11EE8FA5B1EC14006CBA","url":"https://sites.google.com/view/multifp/"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eid8a","text":"Wearing gloves is recommended when working with optical components to avoid damaging optical surface.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9lack","text":"Read manuals of each component before starting. Especially, the cube system DFM1 has important features and strict use. Check this instruction page and the youtube video provided by Thorlabs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":76,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":126,"length":21},{"key":1,"offset":156,"length":13}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2B7162E2251140939238DFC0D9A45008","url":"https://www.thorlabs.com/newgrouppage9.cfm?objectgroup_id=12315"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"62B07A4DA432439790C68E4E16EBC93C","url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qWIfiwuL-gQ"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7pbmu","text":"Although the resultant FIP setup will be a fairly optically closed system, during building experimenter(s) could be exposed to stray light from LEDs. Inappropriate use of any Fiber-Coupled LEDs may result in permanent eye damage. To prevent injury, use the LEDs in accordance with the International Standard “Photobiological Safety of Lamps \u0026 Lamp Systems” IEC 62471. The LEDs used in this system fall under RG2 - Moderate Risk Group in accordance to the standard IEC 62471:2006.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Attach collimator ( F220SMA-532 ) to kinematic collimator mount ( KAD11F ). Similarly to above, avoid protrusion of F220SMA-532 . Then, attach them to SM1L05 . Make two of this assembly.
Attach RS1P to each DFM1 .

Similarly, use O-rings to secure emission filters FF01-520/35 and FF01-630/69 to the insert of the other DFM1 (top left green/red emission cube) to be attached with CMOSs (See Fig. 1 / Fig. 2 ). Again, note arrow on the edge of the filters and match to the light path direction.

Attach four dichroic mirrors to DFM1 s. See Fig. 2 for locations. Also, check the instruction how to use the cube system properly. Pay extra attention not to damage dichroic mirrors. Semrock mirrors should be placed with the Semrock label facing down. Edmund Optics mirrors have a small ink mark/arrow on the mirror edge that points to dichroic coated surface – in this photometry system, they should be placed coating face-up.

Fig. 1. System Overview Fig. 2. Design diagram Schematic items (e.g. 4 cubes, 2 CMOSs, the objective lens, and etc) corresponding to the overview shown in Fig. 1. See the Materials section for products used in the setup. Fig. 3. Spectral features of LEDs, optical filters, and genetic sensor emission signals. a, Normalized intensity of excitation LEDs (L415M, L470M, L565M), and transmission of excitation band-pass filters (FB410, FB470, FB560) and the dual dichroic filter (FF493_574). b, Transmission of the dual dichroic filter (duplicated from a ) separating emission spectrum from excitation spectrum ( Fig. 1 ), and normalized intensity of GCaMP6f and jRGECO1a. Note, this design would be suited for most cpGFP- and cpmApple-based sensors. c, Transmission of the dichroic filter separating green and red emission signals (FF562), and emission band-pass filters (FF01_520_35 for green and FF01_630_69 for red)
Assemble SM1P1 , CP33 , SM1L05 , and AC-254-080 in this order.
Attach 2x ER2-P4 to the top left DFM1 (green/red emission collection cube).

Attach F950SMA-A , a collimator for 560nm, to AD15F . Make sure that F950SMA-A is not protruding from the AD15F. Instead, the backside of both should be at the same level so that it would not damage FB560-10 at the next step. Place FB560-10 inside SM1L05. Make sure for the direction of FB560-10 . Attach the collimator to SM1L05 , and then to the 560 DFM1 (bottom right cube Fig. 1).

Attach two ER025 to each DFM1 orthogonally and remove second screw from each ER025 . Screws need only be attached to the cube face that will connect with an adjacent cube (see Fig. 1.). Each ER025 has two screws, only one is necessary. Two DFM1 s to be connected have to have ER025 attached in the locations so they will be in complementary locations when facing each other in the next step. Yellow arrows - cube faces that require ER025s

CP33 will be used to join the DFM1 s – Attach CP33 to the face of DFM1 s with ER025 s attached. Connect DFM1 s using the CP33 . Connect 4 DFM1 s using three CP33 in total. Pay attention for the direction of DFM1 s as they have directionality (maintain the arrangement established by Fig. 1 and 2 - note the orientation of the Thorlabs logo on each cube). Tighten all screws on CP33 . Close unused holes, where no parts will be installed based on Fig. 1 and 2 , of the DFM1 s using SM1CP2 . This is important to minimize stray light.

Remove insert from bottom left cube (470/415 excitation cube) and use O-rings to secure FBH410-10 and FBH470-10 to DFM1 with proper directionality (note Fig. 1/Fig. 2 ; the direction of the excitation light will match the allow). These are excitation filters for the 415nm and 470nm LEDs – arrow on edge of filter should match the direction of light propagation. Grooves on the O-rings should face up to allow ring to screw into place.
Attach SM1AB2 and SM1A9 to CMOS cameras. Attach them to the main body by sliding in using the ER2 .

Get rid of two rings from SM05T2, and screw SM05T2 into the fiber mount, CXYZ05 . Assemble the fiber bundle with, SM05SMA (or SM05FC for patchcables with a FC connector), SM05L03 , and CXYZ05, and then attach this fiber mount to the main body.
Attach the objective lens using the adapter SM1A12 , and ER4 to the main body.

The assembled main body should look like the fig below. Place the assembled main body on a breadboard and place LEDs ( M415F3, M470F3, M565F3 ) and LED controllers ( LEDD1B ) on the breadboard too. See Fig. 1 .
Connect CMOS cameras to the data acquisition PC using USB cables.

Connect LEDs to collimators on the main body using patch fibers M59L01 . Make sure LED drivers' "CURRENT LIMIT" settings are set to the max current for each LED: M565F3 - 700mA, M415F3 - 1500mA, M470F4 - 1000mA
Arrange 4 cubes ( DFM1 ) 2x2 matching the orientation shown in Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 – pay attention to the position of the Thorlabs logo.
Remove all O-rings from DFM1 and CP33 . Some will be used later.
Attach two of the assembly made in Step#8 to the 415/470 DFM1 (bottom left cube in Fig. 1 ).
Connect CMOS cameras and LED drivers to the TTL generating device (see the associated protocol) using BNC and Hirose cables.

Remove the 493_574 cube insert (top right cube attached to objective) and unscrew the cap ( SM1CP2 ) that blocks stray light from the corresponding DFM1 . Place a white non reflective surface in front of the exposed hole. Turn on the 565nm LED. This light should be projected onto the white surface, ideally a long distance away. Turn on the other LEDs and one at a time, align the angle of propagation of the 415nm and 470nm LEDs to the 565nm LED by adjusting the collimators. Align LEDs

Align fiber bundle and excitation light. The X,Y position of the fiber bundle must be centered on the path of excitation light – otherwise some fibers will be dimmer and others brighter. Insert the 493_574 cube and turn on the 470nm LED to highest power. A bright "hotspot" should be visible projected on the white surface. The shadow of the fiber bundle should also be visible – use the X and Y knobs on the CXYZ05 to center the fiber bundle at this bright hotspot. Do this for each LED.
Set camera settings using Spinview and bring fiber bundle into focus adjusting Z on the CXYZ05 .
Set LED drivers to Trigger mode and test triggering of TTL generating device
creator: Kenta M. Hagihara
